title: Date-range time-off requests (UX upgrade)
spec: User request 2026-06-10: requests should carry real start/end dates instead of a bare day count. Business-day math in the domain; HCM validates the range server-side; the form becomes a date-range picker with a live computed summary and honest validation states; timeline and decision panel show the range.
notes: Sequential: every later phase consumes the domain date module and the widened types.
days stays on the records as the DERIVED business-day count — the projection/hold invariants keep working unchanged; HCM re-derives and rejects mismatches (invalid_range).

Executed 2026-06-10/11. Design decision during phase-2: the wire payload carries ONLY the range — HCM derives the day count itself, eliminating the days-vs-range mismatch class entirely (stronger than validating a client-sent count). MCP visual pass done on the dev server; the e2e suite (production server) covers the full matrix.

phases.title: Domain: business-day ranges
phases.goal: Pure date module (UTC, deterministic): businessDaysBetween, validateRange, formatRange, nextBusinessDay; TimeOffRequest/HcmRequestRecord gain startDate/endDate.

tasks.description: TDD dateRange.ts incl. property tests (weekends excluded, count non-negative, inclusive bounds).
Widen TimeOffRequest + HcmRequestRecord with startDate/endDate; ripple fixtures in domain/data tests.

acceptance: Date math is pure, UTC-based and property-tested

phases.title: Mock HCM + wire: ranges validated server-side
phases.goal: fileRequest accepts startDate/endDate, re-derives the business-day count and rejects mismatches/invalid ranges (new 422 code invalid_range); records carry the range through both transports and the parsers.

tasks.description: TDD store: valid range files with derived days; end<start, weekend-only, or days mismatch → invalid_range; record carries dates.
wire.ts body parsing + parsers.ts record parsing include the dates; route handlers and MSW stay in lockstep.

acceptance: A hostile client cannot file a hold larger or smaller than its range

phases.title: Data layer threading
phases.goal: submitFlow/retry, hcmApi payload, ledger entries and rehydration carry the range; persisted-ledger schema bumps to v2 with a migration dropping pre-date entries.

tasks.description: Update flows + their MSW tests to file with ranges; retry resends the same range.
zustand persist version 2 + migrate (old entries without dates are dropped — they cannot be re-verified into the new shape).

acceptance: No any/casts introduced; rehydration invariants still hold

phases.title: UI/UX: date-range form + range everywhere
phases.goal: RequestForm becomes a start/end picker (native inputs, min=today) with a live summary ('N business days · Jun 15 – Jun 17') and inline validation states (end-before-start, past start, weekend-only); RequestTimeline and DecisionPanel display the range.

tasks.description: TDD RequestForm: valid range submits {locationId, startDate, endDate, days}; each invalid state disables submit and explains why; defaults to the next business day.
RequestTimeline + DecisionPanel show formatRange; component tests updated.
Stories: new validation-state stories for the form; flow-story play functions drive the date inputs.

acceptance: Submit is impossible while the range is invalid, and the reason is on screen
The same range the employee picked is what the manager decides on

phases.title: E2E adaptation + visual verification
phases.goal: E2E helper drives the date inputs (next-Monday anchored ranges); all 16 specs green; Chrome MCP visual pass in light/dark; Chromatic baseline reviewed.

tasks.description: Shared e2e range helper; update all specs that filed via the Days input; full suite green.
MCP visual verification (both views, both schemes, full employee→manager loop) + Chromatic baseline accepted after review; TRD/AGENTS notes.
tasks.verification: Full gate + green CI on main

acceptance: Green CI on main; demo verified visually
